strengths: Strong technical reporting (Power BI, Tableau, Advanced Excel)
Solid SQL and Python ETL experience
Quantified impact on KPI visibility and efficiency
Experience building executive dashboards and presentations
weaknesses: Limited explicit FP&A or finance role experience
No direct financial reporting within shared services/matrixed org
No mention of PowerPoint proficiency examples
Under 3 years in formal financial planning roles
No corporate/monthly reporting or FBP partnership examples
improvement_bullets: Partnered with cross-functional departments in a matrixed environment to deliver automated financial reporting, reducing project delivery delays by 25% and enhancing stakeholder visibility.
Led monthly performance reviews for leadership, translating raw operational data into executive-ready outputs and PowerPoint presentations to support strategic decision-making.
Streamlined monthly reporting workflows by developing custom dashboards, improving the accuracy and agility of financial analysis and reducing manual processing time by 40%.
Collaborated with diverse teams to refine business assumptions and KPIs, providing actionable analytical insights that supported long-term strategy and growth initiatives.
Designed and implemented robust data models and automated reporting templates to support ad hoc analysis, improving operational efficiency and reporting consistency across the organization.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Senior Analyst opportunity with National Grid. I am interested in this opportunity because of National Grid's commitment to delivering sustainable energy and pioneering the flexible energy systems of tomorrow, which aligns perfectly with my drive to leverage data for strategic infrastructure improvements. What interests me about working for National Grid is its commitment to reliability and innovation; this inspires me because I am passionate about using technical analytics to solve complex, real-world logistical and financial challenges. I believe my expertise in data modeling and financial reporting and my strong stakeholder management skills would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by translating complex performance metrics into clear, actionable business strategies. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I identified a need for better financial visibility and developed interactive Power BI dashboards that replaced manual reporting, which resulted in a 40% reduction in data processing time and significantly improved executive decision-making speed. Ultimately, I know that the Senior Analyst role at National Grid would allow me to bring my data visualization and reporting skills to the key responsibility of shaping financial insights that drive business performance. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies (MassMutual) enabled me to develop my matrixed environment collaboration skills. I collaborated cross-functionally with Underwriting, Product, and UI/UX teams to align software features with business objectives, which reduced project delivery delays by 25%. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my proactive, improvement-oriented approach in working on financial planning and analysis at National Grid. I know that my technical proficiency and stakeholder management align with what it takes to succeed in the Senior Analyst role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
